mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 4 Series 418i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned BMW 4 Series 418i engine costs £3,200–£6,000 supply only: N20 (pre-2016) £3,200–£5,500, B48 (2016+) £3,500–£6,000. Fitting adds £900–£1,300. Check the Era Map above for your engine code.

That depends on which engine your 418i has. The N20 (pre-2016) has a well-documented timing chain guide failure risk — this is the defining issue. The B48 (2016+ LCI) is a significant improvement with the chain relocated to the front, virtually eliminating the issue. The LCI B48 is the more reliable choice.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 4 Series 418i?

acceptedAnswer.text: N20: timing chain guide failure (cold-start rattle, £1,500–£3,500). B48: cooling system leaks (water pump, expansion tank, £400–£1,000). Both engines: oil filter housing gasket leaks (£500–£1,200).

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 4 Series 418i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: 4 Series 418i values range from £8,000–£22,000 depending on generation, condition and mileage. A £3,200–£6,000 reconditioned engine represents 15–75% of vehicle value — our 70% BMW scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained LCI example, replacement is often justified. For an ageing N20 with other issues, assess scrap value first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 4 Series 418i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 418i has used two engines: N20B20 (2013–2016, 136–143bhp) and B48B20 (2016–2020, 184bhp). Check your VIN or registration year — pre-2016 is N20, 2016+ is B48. The code is stamped on the engine block.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N20 timing chain issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. Timing chain and guide replacement costs £1,500–£3,500 and is significantly cheaper than a full engine replacement (£3,200–£5,500). BMW issued a recall on some N20 models. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately — ignored chain wear can lead to catastrophic piston-valve contact.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW 4 Series 418i — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Replacement often £3,500–£5,500 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW 4 Series 418i? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-2016 LCI (B48) — the more reliable engine by a clear margin
* N20 (pre-2016) with documented timing chain history (guides and tensioner replaced)
* Full BMW service history with correct oil specification for the era
* Any 418i with low-mileage and documented history
avoidIf: * Pre-2016 N20 with no timing chain history — the risk is real
* Any evidence of coolant loss (B48) or cold-start rattle (N20)
* Poor service history — both engines are unforgiving of neglect
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — these engines can hide expensive problems

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 418i is the entry-point to 4 Series ownership, and the choice between N20 and B48 is stark. The B48 (post-2016 LCI) is a significantly more reliable engine — chain front-mounted, cooling system improved. The N20 is cheaper but has the well-documented timing chain risk. Buy the LCI, maintain the cooling system, and the 418i will serve you well.

cta.label: → Compare BMW 4 Series 418i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 4 Series 418i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Any rattle? N20 chain rattle is the biggest warning; B48 should be quiet
* Service History — Check the year — pre-2016 = N20, 2016+ = B48
* Timing Chain History — N20: chains replaced? Tensioner checked? (pre-2015 is critical)
* Coolant Level — B48: any loss? Water pump and expansion tank are key risks
* Oil Filter Housing — Any oil leaks at the front of the engine? (both engines)
* Turbo Response — Test acceleration; any hesitation or lag?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Resolve before buying
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
